extra.message: SQL Injection vulnerability in Student.create(). User input from 'name' parameter is directly interpolated into the SQL query using Python string formatting ('%(name)s'). An attacker can inject malicious SQL through the name field to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

Potential SQL Injection in Course.get_many(). The method uses dictionary unpacking (**params) instead of tuple passing. When limit/offset are provided, the query string is constructed dynamically and params are passed incorrectly to cur.execute().

Insecure password hashing using MD5. The check_password() method uses MD5 hash algorithm which is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks. Passwords should use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort
CWE-327: Use of Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability. The 'course.description' is rendered without HTML escaping (no '|e' filter). With autoescape=False in app configuration, malicious JavaScript in course descriptions will execute in users' browsers.

Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability. The 'review.review_text' is rendered without HTML escaping (no '|e' filter). With autoescape=False in app configuration, malicious JavaScript in review text will execute in users' browsers.

Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability. The 'errors' dictionary values are rendered directly without HTML escaping. Error messages containing user input could execute arbitrary JavaScript.
CSRF protection is disabled. The csrf_middleware is commented out in the middlewares list, leaving all POST endpoints vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks.

Session cookie is not HttpOnly. RedisStorage is initialized with httponly=False, allowing client-side JavaScript to access the session cookie. This makes the application more vulnerable to XSS attacks.

Autoescaping is disabled in Jinja2 configuration (autoescape=False). This leaves the application vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks as all template variables will be rendered as raw HTML.

cwe: CWE-116: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output

extra.message: Missing authentication on students endpoint. The students() view allows POST requests to create students without requiring authentication. No @authorize() decorator is applied.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication on courses endpoint. The courses() view allows POST requests to create courses without requiring authentication. No @authorize() decorator is applied.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing authentication on review endpoint. The review() view allows POST requests to create reviews without requiring authentication. No @authorize() decorator is applied.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Missing admin authorization check. The logout() view has @authorize() decorator but doesn't check for admin privileges when logging out. While logout is generally allowed for any authenticated user, the decorator should be explicit.

Information disclosure in error handling. The template exposes error.__dict__ which may leak sensitive internal application state including stack traces, configuration values, or other debugging information.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message
CWE-532: Information Exposure Through Log Files

extra.message: Debug mode is enabled (debug=True). This can expose sensitive information such as stack traces and environment details in error messages and should not be used in production.
Hardcoded database credentials found. The configuration file contains hardcoded username 'postgres' and password 'postgres' which should be loaded from environment variables or a secrets manager.
